segments.s581-g1: A nominal phrase in normal Persian order: two adjectives coordinated before the name. خردمند و شایسته = 'wise and worthy' modify بهرامشاه (Bahramshah) as a single noun phrase.
segments.s581-g2: همی داشت is a past continuous/perfective progressive construction: همی is a literary continuous marker and داشت is the past-tense auxiliary meaning 'kept / was holding' (literally 'had'). سوک پدر is the object 'the father's mourning' and چندگاه is an adverb 'for a while', placed at the end.
segments.s581-g3: چو (when) introduces a temporal clause and precedes the past verb بنشست ('he sat down'). بر جایگاه مهی is a prepositional phrase: بر + noun جایگاه ('seat/place') modified by the adjective مهی ('moon-like' or 'radiant'), following the noun in Persian word order.
segments.s581-g4: چنین (thus) is an adverbial modifier placed before the verb. گفت here functions as the 3rd-person past of گفتن ('he said' / 'he spoke') — the provided senses list did not include 'said', so the appropriate lexical sense is not available in the supplied options. بر تخت شاهنشهی is another prepositional phrase ('on the imperial throne') with تخت as head noun and شاهنشهی as an attributive adjective.
segments.s581-g5: که introduces a content/relative clause 'that' / 'who'. هر شاه = 'every king'. کز is a contracted combination of که + ز (i.e. 'who/from whom'), here meaning 'from whom' or 'whose', introducing the source phrase داد ('justice'). گنج آگند is a small clause 'treasure (is) filled' — آگند is a past participle/adjective 'filled' describing گنج, so the whole phrase means 'every king whose treasure is filled by justice'.
segments.s581-g6: بدانید is an imperative or jussive 'know' / 'be aware'. کان here is an archaic/demonstrative particle meaning 'that' (the supplied senses do not match this function), introducing the clause about the treasure. گنج is the noun 'treasure' and نپراگند is a negative past-form/adjective meaning 'was not scattered' / 'remained intact', so the clause commands the audience to know that the treasure was not dispersed.
segments.s581-g7: ز ما = a poetic prepositional phrase meaning 'for/with us' (زو = از + ما). ایزد پاک is the noun + adjective 'pure God' and خشنود باد is an optative construction: خشنود (predicate adjective) + باد (optative particle) = 'may [he] be pleased'. Word order is typical classical Persian: prepositional phrase first, then the subject phrase, then the optative verb-particle.
segments.s581-g8: بداندیش را topicalizes/marks the targeted person (بداندیش) with را before the predicate. دل پرِ از دود is a noun phrase 'a heart full of smoke' where پر (full) takes از + noun to mean 'filled with X'. The final باد is again the optative particle 'may', so the whole clause means 'may the malicious one's heart be full of smoke.'
segments.s581-g65: بزرگی و دانش is a coordinated noun phrase ('greatness and knowledge'). ورا is the dative/benefactive pronoun 'for him' (classical/poetic form of برای او or از او depending on context) marking who benefits; راه is the predicate noun 'way/path' that states what those qualities will be for him; باد is the optative particle 'may it be' placed at the end (poetic word order), so the whole segment reads literally 'greatness and knowledge for him way may be' → 'May greatness and knowledge be his way.'
segments.s581-g66: وزو here is a poetic/contractional form meaning 'and from him' (literary 'و زِ او') introducing the next clause; دست بدخواه is a genitive construction 'the hand of the ill‑wisher' where the ezāfe is omitted in poetic diction; کوتاه is the predicate adjective 'short' used figuratively of power ('be cut short'/'be powerless'); باد again is the optative 'may it be', so the clause means 'and may the ill‑wisher’s hand be shortened (i.e. be powerless toward him)'.
segments.s581-g49: This is a vocative/nominal phrase introduced by the interrogative/exclamatory particle ایا, addressing someone by age. شست و سه ساله is a compound numeral phrase + ساله, functioning as an adjectival phrase '…‑year‑old' modifying مرد (man); کهن is a postpositive adjective 'old/ancient' placed after the noun, so the whole chunk = 'O sixty‑three‑year‑old old man.'
segments.s581-g50: تو is the addressed 'you'. از … تا is a correlative prepositional span 'from … until/for' and چند is the interrogative 'how long/how much' querying the extent; سخن is the noun 'speech/words' at the end. رانی here is not represented in the provided sense list: in the poem it functions as a verb addressing the addressee (second person), so the phrase asks how long you will continue (literally 'drive/go') in relation to the wind; overall the clause asks 'from the wind until when will you… (speak)?' The precise lexical sense of رانی is archaic/poetic and not in the supplied senses.
segments.s581-g51: همان روز 'that same day' is a noun phrase fronted for emphasis. تو is again the addressee. ناگهان 'suddenly' is an adverb modifying the verb. بگذرد is a present/subjunctive or jussive third‑person form of گذر(یدن) 'to pass' (classical usage can express sudden passing or dying); here it describes the sudden passing/ending associated with that day.
segments.s581-g52: در توبه is a prepositional phrase 'into/in repentance' where توبه is a noun 'repentance'. بگزین is the second‑person imperative (or hortative) of گزیدن 'to choose' meaning 'choose (it)'. و links the two coordinated objects/commands; راه خرد is a noun phrase 'the path of wisdom' where خرد is a postpositive modifier 'wisdom'. The clause issues a command: 'choose repentance and the way of wisdom.'
segments.s581-g53: جهاندار is the noun 'the ruler/sovereign' (subject or addressee). زین پیر is a short prepositional/nominal phrase modifying whom the wish concerns ('this old man'); زین here is an archaic/deictic form and does not behave as a verb. خشنود is a predicative adjective 'pleased', and باد is the classical optative particle 'may' placed after the predicate to make a wish ('may be pleased'). The word order is Subject + (phrase about whom) + predicate + optative, producing 'May the ruler be pleased with this old man.'
segments.s581-g54: This is two coordinated optative clauses. خرد مایه باد is a nominal clause where خرد 'wisdom' is the theme and مایه 'resource/substance' is the predicative noun; باد again is the optative 'may' ('may wisdom be a resource'). و links the second coordinated clause: سخن سود باد means 'may speech be benefit' with سخن 'speech' as theme, سود 'benefit' as predicative noun, and باد the optative particle. The copula is omitted and expressed by باد in each clause.
segments.s581-g55: اگر introduces a conditional protasis ('if ...'). در سخن is an adverbial phrase 'in speech' or 'in what he says'. موی is a direct-object noun 'a hair' (here likely a minimal/trifling point). کافد is a third-person singular verbal form in classical Persian (root کافت/کافَت); its exact lexical meaning is uncertain here, but grammatically it is the verb of the if-clause. همی is the older continuous/habitual verbal particle that follows the verb phrase, marking an ongoing or habitual action.
segments.s581-g56: به تاریکی اندر is a direction/goal phrase 'into darkness' (به ... اندر = into/into the state of). ببافد is the classical present/habitual verbal form of بافتن 'to weave' (the prefixed ب- appears in older narratives), and together with همی (again marking continuous/habitual) it yields an apodosis: 'he weaves/keeps weaving [it] into darkness.' The clause is the result clause of the preceding اگر conditional.
segments.s581-g17: 'وگر' introduces a conditional (“if”/“but if”). 'چند' modifies 'بخشی' meaning “a few / several,” and 'بخشی' is a noun “portion.” 'ز' is the poetic preposition “from,” so 'ز گنج سخن' means “from the treasure of speech/words” (genitive phrase: treasure of speech). The phrase as a whole sets up: “if (you give) a few portions from the treasure of words…”.
segments.s581-g18: 'برافشان' is an injunctive/imperative-form verb (literary) meaning “scatter”/“disperse.” 'که' here introduces purpose/explanation (“so that / so the result is that”). 'دانش' is the object “knowledge.” 'نیاید' is the negative present/subjunctive form meaning “may not / should not come.” 'به بن' is a locative phrase “to the root/base,” the goal of 'نیاید.' Together: “scatter (them) so that knowledge will not reach the root.”
segments.s581-g19: 'ز' here means “concerning/with respect to.” 'نیک و بدیها' is the phrase “good and bad (things).” 'به یزدان گرای' is an instruction: 'به' = “to/toward,” 'یزدان' = “God,” and 'گرای' (literary) functions as an imperative/verb meaning “turn/ incline (toward).” The whole segment: “With respect to good and evil, turn to God.”
segments.s581-g20: 'چو' here = conditional 'if.' 'خواهی' is the second-person singular present “you wish/want.” 'که' introduces the content clause (“that …”). 'نیکیت' = 'نیکی' + possessive suffix, “your goodness/virtue.” 'ماند' is the present/subjunctive meaning “remain/last.” 'به جای' = “in place / in effect.” Together: “if you wish that your goodness remain in its place.”
segments.s581-g21: اگر begins the conditional. ز و شناسی here represents the fused archaic 'زو' (= از + او) 'from him / of him'; شناسی functions as a present/subjunctive form 'you know / you discern' (classical usage). همه modifies the coordinated phrase خوب و زشت = 'all the good and the bad.' The clause is the protasis ('if' clause).
segments.s581-g22: بیابی is a subjunctive/optative form (classical 2nd-person form used in the apodosis after اگر) meaning 'you will find / you will attain.' به پاداش... is a prepositional phrase 'as a reward' where خرم ('joyful') qualifies بهشت ('paradise'). The whole segment is the consequence: 'you will attain joyful paradise as reward.'
segments.s581-g23: وگر = 'but if' (conditional). برگزینی is a second-person present/subjunctive form derived from برگزین 'choose' and here means 'if you choose.' ز گیتی هوا = 'the desire of the world' (ز = 'of/from', گیتی = 'world', هوا = 'desire'), a poetic word order placing the genitive first.
segments.s581-g24: بمانی is the apodosis verb (2nd-person present/subjunctive) meaning 'you will remain / you shall be left.' به چنگ = 'into the clutch/grip of' (چنگ literally 'claw, talon' used metaphorically) + هوا 'desire' repeats the notion of passion; بی‌نوا is a predicate adjective 'miserable / pitiable.' Together: 'you will remain in the grasp of desire, miserable.'
segments.s581-g9: همه دانش اوراست literally places the quantifier 'همه' before the noun 'دانش' (all knowledge) and follows with the fused predicate 'اوراست' — an older/poetic form meaning 'it is his' (او + راست). Then 'ما بنده‌ایم' is a simple present copular clause: 'ما' (we) + 'بنده‌ایم' (we are servants). The clause order is topic → predicate, typical of Persian poetic style.
segments.s581-g10: The subordinating particle 'که' introduces a relative/explanatory clause ('who/that'). 'کاهنده' and 'فزاینده' are participial/adjectival forms (diminishing / increasing) linked to the copula '-ایم' (we are) at the end. 'هم' here means 'also' or 'both', so the phrase says 'we are (both) diminishing and increasing' — two coordinated predicates describing 'ما'.
segments.s581-g11: 'جهاندار یزدان' is an appositive noun phrase: 'جهاندار' (ruler of the world) with 'یزدان' (God) naming who that ruler is. 'بود' is the past copula 'was'. The nouns 'داد' and 'راست' function as predicate qualities (justice / uprightness) — a compact poetic way of saying 'was just and upright.'
segments.s581-g12: The conjunction/relative 'که' links this clause to the previous. 'نفزود' is a past form with negative prefix (here 'ن‑') meaning 'did not increase.' 'در پادشاهی' is a locative/field phrase 'in the kingdom,' and 'نه کاست' places a second negative predicate at the end: 'nor did he decrease' (کاست = diminished, past). The structure expresses a balanced negation: he neither increased nor decreased the realm.
segments.s581-g13: 'کسی کو' is an archaic/poetic relative construction meaning 'anyone who' (here 'کو' functions like a relative 'who'). 'به بخشش' uses 'به' to mark the domain/area ('in/by giving' = 'in generosity') and 'توانا بود' is the past copula construction 'was capable/able.' The whole means 'anyone who was generous/able in giving.'
segments.s581-g14: A simple predicate list: three adjectives joined by 'و' ('خردمند' wise, 'بیدار' awake/alert, 'دانا' learned) followed by the past copula 'بود' ('was'). In Persian it is normal for multiple adjectival predicates to follow the subject and share the final verb.
segments.s581-g15: 'نباید' is an impersonal modal meaning 'it should not / must not (be that...)'. After it, 'که' introduces the subordinate verb clause. 'بندد' is the (present/subjunctive-form) verb 'to bind/close' used here with the sense 'to lock up'; after a prohibitive/modal like 'نباید' Persian often uses this form. 'در گنج سخت' is locative: 'in/into a strong/secure treasure (vault),' with 'سخت' functioning adverbially or attributively to imply 'firmly/securely.'
segments.s581-g16: 'به ویژه' means 'especially.' Here it qualifies the following nouns: 'خداوند' (lord/sovereign) followed by 'دیهیم' (an adjective 'rural/of the district' used postpositively) and then 'و تخت' ('and the throne') coordinated by 'و'. The noun+adjective order (noun followed by its modifier) is common in poetic/older Persian.
segments.s581-g25: چو is a temporal/conditional conjunction here ('when'/'if'). داردت is the present verb دارد ('he/it has/holds') with the clitic -ت marking 'you' as object: 'he holds you' or 'is with you'. یزدان is the subject (God). بدو = به + او, a prepositional pronoun 'to him' (i.e. to God). دست یاز is an imperative phrase: دست ('hand') + یاز (an imperative stem meaning 'stretch/reach/open'), literally 'reach out your hand'.
segments.s581-g26: بدان here functions as an adverbial/result marker ('by that' / 'thereby'); it is written as ب + دان in the segmentation but acts as 'thereby/with that'. تا introduces the purpose/result clause ('so that' / 'in order that'). نمانی is present-subjunctive/negative of ماندن with the 2nd‑person singular suffix, 'you do not remain'. به introduces the state/condition ('in' / 'into'), followed by گرم و گداز describing the condition: گرم 'heat, warmth' and گداز an emotional or burning anguish ('ardor/gnawing grief').
segments.s581-g27: چنین is a demonstrative/adverb meaning 'thus' or 'such'. است is the copula 'is'. امیدم is 'my hope' (امید + possessive -م). به یزدان پاک is a prepositional phrase 'in/to the pure God' where به marks the locus/target of the hope and پاک qualifies یزدان.
segments.s581-g28: که introduces a content clause ('that'). چون here functions as a temporal conjunction ('when' / 'since') leading into what follows. سر بیارم literally 'I bring my head' — a poetic idiom for laying down one's head (i.e. dying or being buried); بیارم is a verb form 'I bring/put' (the segmentation's lemma is anomalous, so the verb sense is given below). بدین is ب + این 'to this' (= 'into this'), and تیره‌خاک 'dark earth' = the grave/earth; together: 'that when I lay my head in this dark earth.'
segments.s581-g29: جهاندار پیروز is a nominal phrase (titles: 'lord of the world, the Victorious') functioning as the subject. دارد is the present verb 'has/holds' (here = 'protects/sustains'). مرا is the object 'me' (in the segmentation split into morphemes; را appears as a separate clitic marking definiteness/emphasis). The clause asserts that the sovereign protects the speaker.
segments.s581-g30: همان 'that very' (or 'the same') introduces the next title. گیتی‌افروز (here split as گیتی + افروز) is an epithet/name meaning 'world‑illuminator' or 'world‑brightener' (a divine epithet). دارد مرا repeats the verb + object pattern: 'holds/keeps me.' The two short clauses are parallel, doubling the divine protectors' titles.
segments.s581-g31: گر is the conditional 'if'. اندر جهان 'in the world' (اندر = in/within). داد here is the noun 'justice' rather than a past-tense verb, so the phrase reads 'if I scatter/spread justice in the world'. بپراگنم is a present-subjunctive first-person form meaning 'I scatter/spread' (ب- verbal prefix + پراگنـ stem + -م).
segments.s581-g32: ازان به که is a comparative formula 'than that' / 'better than (that)'. ازان = 'from that' / 'than that'. به که introduces the contrasted clause. بیداد is 'injustice/oppression'; گنج 'treasure'; آگنم (here from آگند/آگین) means 'I fill' or 'make full' (so 'filling the treasure'). The whole segment contrasts spreading justice with filling one's treasury by injustice.
segments.s581-g57: گر introduces a conditional ('if'). The short clause order is conditional particle + subject (او) + demonstrative + noun phrase; که introduces a relative clause modifying این سخن‌ها. اندرگرفت is the perfect/simple past (third singular) of the compound اندر گرفتن 'to take up / to seize (one's attention)', here functioning inside the relative clause 'which he took up/was seized by'.
segments.s581-g58: به پیری is a prepositional phrase 'into/unto old age' (به = 'into/towards'). سرآرد is a verb form meaning 'bring (to)/cause to come' (literally 'brings to the head/occurs'), used here with پیری as its goal. نباشد شگفت is an impersonal/clausal predicate meaning 'there is no wonder / it is not surprising' — نباشد is the negative of باش (optative/subjunctive style) and شگفت functions as 'wonder' (what would be surprising).
segments.s581-g59: به نام is a prepositional phrase 'in the name of' (به here = 'in/by'), followed by the genitive noun شهنشاه; the phrase precedes شمشیرزن and functions as an epithet or specification of the swordsman ('the swordsman, in the name of the Shahanshah' / 'the swordsman by the Shahanshah's name').
segments.s581-g60: به بالا is an adverbial phrase 'upwards / high up'. سرش is 'his head' (سر + possessive clitic -ش). برتر از is the comparative 'higher than / superior to', with از marking the standard of comparison (انجمن = 'the assembly'). The segment is verbless/nominal here and so attaches to the preceding action (poetically implying he held the sword high).
segments.s581-g61: زمانه here functions as the wished-for subject ('the times' or 'fortune'). به کام = 'to the desire of / for the satisfaction of' (به = 'to/for', کام = 'desire/wish'). باد at the end is the optative particle 'may it be' (a fixed formulaic wish). The whole segment is a conventional benediction: 'May the times be to the Shahanshah's wish.'
segments.s581-g62: سر تخت او = 'the top/head of his throne' (سر = 'top/summit' here, تخت = 'throne', او = 'his'). افسر ماه is a noun compound 'a crown/helmet like the moon' (افسر = crown/helmet, ماه = 'moon' used figuratively for a bright/beautiful ornament). باد again is the optative 'may it be', so the clause reads 'May a moon-like crown be upon the head of his throne.'
segments.s581-g63: کزویست is a fused form meaning 'that which is from him / from him is' (کز = 'from/that from', ویست = 'is from him'). The construction places the predicate (کام 'desire') after it: 'from him is desire'. و links to the next noun; بدویست here functions adjectivally/verbally as 'is primordial/ancient' or 'originally is', modifying نام 'name/reputation'. The overall sense is a two-part assertion about what derives from him: desire and an ancient name.
segments.s581-g64: ورا باد is another optative benediction phrase 'may it be to him / may he have'. تاج کیی is a genitive construction 'the Kayanian crown' (تاج = crown, کیی = Kayanian). شادکام is an adjective 'fortunate / joyful / fulfilled' qualifying the hoped-for possession. Grammatically the sequence is 'May the Kayanian crown be his, (making him) joyful/fortunate.'
segments.s581-g41: به یک چندگه is a temporal prepositional phrase meaning 'for a short/one while' modifying the clause. دیر here is an adverb meaning 'long (of time)' and combines with بیمار بود ('was ill') to form the past-state predicate 'was ill for a long time.' Word order is topical ('for a while') + adverb + predicate.
segments.s581-g42: دل کهتران is a noun phrase 'the heart of the older one(s)' (دل = 'heart'); پر ز + تیمار is an adjectival phrase 'full of care' where پر ('full (of)') takes ز ('of/from') and the noun تیمار ('care/solicitude'). بود is the past copula 'was.' The construction means 'the heart was filled with care.'
segments.s581-g43: نبودش is a negative existential phrasing meaning 'there was not for him / he did not have.' It precedes the noun پسر ('son') to state 'he had no son.' The next clause پنج دخترش بود is a neutral predication 'five daughters he had' (numeral + possessed noun + past copula).
segments.s581-g44: این جمله is a simple comparative clause: یکی ('one') is the subject, کهتر ('smaller/younger') with از وی ('than him') makes the comparison, and برادرش بود is the predicate 'was his brother.' Word order: subject — comparative phrase — predicate ('one, younger than him, was his brother').
segments.s581-g45: بدو is the dative enclitic meaning 'to him/unto him' and precedes the verb داد ('gave') in the simple past. ناگاه is an adverb 'suddenly' modifying the giving. گنج و سپاه are the direct objects 'treasure and army/troops.'
segments.s581-g46: همان مهر شاهی is a nominal phrase where همان = 'that/the same' and مهر شاهی = 'royal favour' (مهر here = 'favour/kindness', شاهی = 'royal/kingship' used adjectivally). The following و تخت و کلاه lists the regalia 'and the throne and the cap/crown.' The phrase restates the nature of what was given.
segments.s581-g47: جهاندار برنا is a compound nominal phrase 'the youthful/world-ruling one' (جهاندار = 'sovereign, world-ruler'; برنا = 'young/vigorous'). ز گیتی is the prepositional phrase 'from the world/earth' and برفت is the past verb 'departed/left' (poetic past of رفتن with the prefix بر-). Together: 'the young sovereign left the world.'
segments.s581-g48: This is a temporal-clause summarizing elapsed time. سالیان = 'years'; برگذشته is a past-participial/adverbial sense 'having passed/elapsed.' دو هفت here is an idiomatic numeral (two sevens = fourteen). The whole phrase means 'and fourteen years had gone by.'
segments.s581-g33: که is the subordinating conjunction introducing a conditional/causal clause ('if/that'). ایدر is an adverb meaning 'here'. بماند is third-person singular present/subjunctive form of ماندن ('remain' / 'be left') used in a hypothetical sense; the verb appears before its noun phrase (poetic inversion). همه رنج ما is a noun phrase 'all our suffering' with the possessive pronoun ما after the noun (typical Persian order).
segments.s581-g34: به + noun marks the indirect object/goal ('to the enemy'). رسد is third-person singular of رسیدن used here in the sense 'reach/come into (someone's possession)' and has a predictive/habitual force. بی‌گمان is an adverb 'undoubtedly' modifying the clause. گنج ما is 'our treasure' (noun + possessive) and follows the verb by poetic word order.
segments.s581-g35: که again introduces a subordinate clause ('that/so that'). تخت بزرگی is 'the great throne' (noun + quality expressed by بزرگی). نماند is the negative form of ماندن here meaning 'be left/remain (to)'; the phrase نماند به کس means 'remain to no one' or 'be left to nobody' (verb before the oblique 'به کس' in poetic order).
segments.s581-g36: جهاندار 'sovereign/world-ruler' is the noun; باشد is the subjunctive/optative form 'may there be' or 'let there be' (a wish/optative). ت + را together form ترا ('to/for you') — in the line they are split into the second-person clitic (ت) and the clitic را, marking the oblique object 'for you'. یار بس is a verbless predicate 'a single companion/ally is enough' with بس functioning as the predicate 'enough'. The whole chunk expresses a wish that a sovereign be yours and that one ally suffices.
segments.s581-g37: بد و نیک are coordinated nouns 'bad and good' (both sorts). ماند is third-person singular of ماندن ('will remain' / 'remains'). ز ما is the poetic preposition meaning 'from us' (modern از). یادگار is a predicative noun 'a memorial' or 'memento' — the clause says 'good and bad remain from us as a memorial.'
segments.s581-g38: تو is the vocative/emphatic 'you'. تخم بدی is a noun phrase 'seed of evil' (تخم 'seed' modified by بدی 'evil'). تا introduces a extent clause 'as far as / to the extent that' and governs the subjunctive توانى ('you can / you are able'). مکار here functions as an exhortation ('be deceitful' / 'deceiver') — in poetic usage it can be read as an imperative/adjective addressing the addressee.
segments.s581-g39: چو شد is a temporal clause 'when it became / when it came'. سال is 'year'. آن پادشا 'that king' is a genitival/attributive phrase qualifying the year. بر here marks attainment/reaching ('to/on') and دو هفت ('two sevens' = fourteen) is a poetic numeral expression; together 'سال ... بر دو هفت' means 'the king's year reached two sevens.'
segments.s581-g40: به + پالیز marks motion/direction 'to/into the orchard'. آن سرو یازان is 'that cypress (yazān = spreading/opening)' with یازان an adjectival/participial form describing the cypress. بخفت is an archaic/poetic past form of خفتن meaning 'he slept / he lay down to sleep.' Word order places the locative phrase before the verb, a common poetic inversion.

sentences.s581-s1: The wise and worthy Bahramshah kept his father's mourning for a while.
sentences.s581-s2: When he sat on the moon-like seat, thus he spoke upon the imperial throne.
sentences.s581-s3: For know that every king whose treasure is filled by justice — that treasure was not scattered.
sentences.s581-s4: May the pure God be pleased with us; may the malicious one's heart be filled with smoke.
sentences.s581-s33: May greatness and knowledge be his path, and may the ill‑wisher’s hand be shortened.
sentences.s581-s25: O sixty‑three‑year‑old aged man, how long will you speak of the wind?
sentences.s581-s26: On that same day you will suddenly pass away; choose repentance and the path of wisdom.
sentences.s581-s27: May the sovereign be pleased with this old man; may wisdom be his resource and may speech be of benefit.
sentences.s581-s28: If in his speech he were to seize a hair, he would (ever) weave it into darkness.
sentences.s581-s9: But if you dispense a few portions from the treasure of words, scatter them so that knowledge may not reach the root.
sentences.s581-s10: Turn to God concerning good and evil, if you wish that your goodness remain in its place.
sentences.s581-s11: If you discern from him all that is good and bad, you will attain joyful paradise as your reward.
sentences.s581-s12: But if you choose worldly desire, you will remain in the clutch of desire, miserable.
sentences.s581-s5: All knowledge belongs to Him; we are His servants who both lessen and increase.
sentences.s581-s6: God, the sovereign of the world, was just and true; he neither increased nor diminished the kingdom.
sentences.s581-s7: Whoever was able in giving was wise, alert, and learned.
sentences.s581-s8: One should not lock treasure in a strong vault, especially (that of) the local lord and the throne.
sentences.s581-s13: When God holds you, stretch out your hand to Him so that you do not remain in heat and anguish.
sentences.s581-s14: Such is my hope in the pure God: that when I lay my head in this dark earth...
sentences.s581-s15: The sovereign, the Victorious, holds me; that same world‑illuminator holds me.
sentences.s581-s16: If I were to spread justice in the world, that would be better than my filling the treasure with injustice.
sentences.s581-s29: If he carries these words which he took up into old age, it would not be surprising.
sentences.s581-s30: In the name of the Shahanshah, the swordsman — with (it) high above his head, higher than the assembly.
sentences.s581-s31: May the times be to the Shahanshah's desire; may a moon-like crown be upon the head of his throne.
sentences.s581-s32: From him comes desire and ancient renown; may the Kayanian crown be his and bring him joy.
sentences.s581-s21: For a while he had long been ill; the elder's heart was full of care.
sentences.s581-s22: He had no son; he had five daughters — one was younger than him and was his brother.
sentences.s581-s23: Suddenly he gave him treasure and troops — that royal favour, the throne and the crown.
sentences.s581-s24: The youthful world-ruler departed from the earth; fourteen years had passed.
sentences.s581-s17: If all our hardships remain here, our treasure will surely fall into the enemy's hands.
sentences.s581-s18: That no great throne remain to anyone; may the sovereign be yours — one ally is enough for you.
sentences.s581-s19: Both bad and good will remain from us as a memorial; you, sow the seed of evil as far as you can — be deceitful.
sentences.s581-s20: When that king's year came to two sevens, he lay down to sleep in the orchard by that spreading cypress.
